hypotheses_considered.summary: Insert a tiny legal anchor4 front-z closed pair before the corrected entry relay. This is a fresh corrected-basis family: earlier than the corrected anchor7/8/9 basin, contiguous with the native 4→5 front corridor, and not part of the already-pruned corrected anchor2/3/startup families.

Insert a tiny exact-return anchor6 inner y-pair before the corrected entry relay. This targets the 6→7 handoff while leaving the successful late relay structure untouched, and is structurally different from the already-pruned corrected gateway diagonal / butterfly families.

Open a small mixed-beta diagonal pair at anchor4 before the corrected entry relay. Still plausible, but lower-priority than a pure front-z precursor because the corrected anchor3 mirror family already showed early mixed-beta motion is risky.

Couple a tiny anchor4 front-z precursor with a tiny anchor6 handoff perturbation on top of the corrected entry relay. Held back because both isolated links should be read out first; otherwise it risks reopening two weak basins at once under a new name.

selected_families: anchor4 front-z precursor on corrected entry backbone
anchor6 handoff y-pair on corrected entry backbone

rows_sorted.rationale: Smallest anchor4 front-z precursor on the corrected entry backbone. Tests whether a contiguous front-half z preconditioner can soften max without reopening the heavier anchor5 basin.

Gentlest anchor6 inner y-pair before the corrected entry relay. Theory: a tiny handoff perturbation may condition the later x-negative corridor without directly disturbing the winning late relay geometry.
Mildly stronger anchor4 front-z precursor, still far below the old heavy front-corridor doses.
Dose-up anchor4 front-z precursor on the corrected entry backbone.
Slightly stronger anchor6 handoff y-pair on the corrected entry backbone.

best_candidate.rationale: Smallest anchor4 front-z precursor on the corrected entry backbone. Tests whether a contiguous front-half z preconditioner can soften max without reopening the heavier anchor5 basin.

kf36_gate_reason: No candidate reached the competitiveness gate.

bottom_line.statement: No new corrected-basis takeover emerged. The best landing does not beat the incumbent or the splitbookend runner-up, but it does carve out a sharper front-half max-oriented side branch: `frontz_l4_neg1_on_entry` beats the corrected x-boundary branch on median and max while giving back only 0.014 mean-points.
